title: Generating Text Step-by-Step
concept: RNN generators start with a seed prompt, predict a probability distribution over the vocabulary, sample a word, and feed it back to the next step.

exercises.prompt: How do Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) naturally generate text?
exercises.explain: RNNs process sequential data step-by-step. Generating text is done by predicting the next word sequentially, making RNNs a perfect fit.

cards.title: Predicting the Next Word
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how AI autocomplete on your phone seems to read your mind, suggesting one word after another? It uses a clever loop that predicts the future by feeding its own outputs back into itself!

cards.title: Step-by-Step Generation
cards.body: An RNN generator creates text word-by-word. It starts with a starter phrase called a **seed prompt**. For each step, it looks at the words it has so far, calculates the probability for every word in its vocabulary, picks (samples) one, and feeds it back as the input for the next step.

cards.title: The Jazz Improviser
cards.body: Imagine a jazz musician improvising a melody. They don't write the whole song at once. Instead, they play one note (the seed), listen to it, decide what note sounds best next based on that, play it, and then use both notes to decide the third one. It's a continuous, rolling loop!

cards.title: Watching it in Action
cards.body:
Let's say the seed prompt is "The cat". 

1. The RNN looks at "The cat" and predicts probabilities for the next word (e.g., "sat": 60%, "slept": 30%, "banana": 0.1%).
2. It samples "sat".
3. Now, the new input is "The cat sat". 
4. It predicts the next word: "on" (80%), "happily" (10%).
5. It samples "on" and repeats the process!

cards.title: Do They Plan Ahead?
cards.body:
**Myth:** Text generators plan out the entire sentence or paragraph before they start writing.
**Fact:** RNNs live in the moment! They generate text one single token or word at a time, relying on the loop to keep the story consistent.

cards.title: The Generation Loop
cards.body: See how the output of the RNN travels back to become the input for the next cycle.

right.text: Predicts probabilities, samples a word, and sends it back to the input.

left.text: Takes the 'seed prompt' or the accumulated text generated so far.

exercises.prompt: What is the name of the initial text given to an RNN to kick off the generation process?
exercises.explain: The initial text you give an RNN to start the generation process is called the 'seed prompt'. Without it, the network wouldn't have a starting point to generate the next word.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An RNN text generator must always choose the word with the absolute highest probability, with no randomness allowed.
exercises.explain: RNNs do not just pick the single absolute highest probability word every time; they sample from a probability distribution to allow for creativity and variety.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how RNNs reuse their own outputs.
exercises.sentence: RNNs use a feedback loop where each generated word becomes the next input.
exercises.explain: This loop is called a feedback loop because the output of one step (the chosen word) is fed back in as the input for the very next step.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes an RNN text generator at work?

options.text: An AI categorizes an email as "Spam" or "Inbox".
An AI writes "The robot walked" one word at a time starting from "The".

exercises.explain: Only 'The robot walked' represents a generator outputting text step-by-step. The others are classification and translation tasks.

exercises.prompt: An RNN is writing a story. It has just generated the word 'sky' after the seed 'The blue'. What is the very next thing the RNN does to continue the story?

options.text: It predicts all words in the sentence simultaneously to save time.
It randomly shuffles a list of words until they make grammatical sense.
It predicts the next word, selects one, and feeds it back as input for the next step.
It looks up pre-written sentences in a database based on the seed.

exercises.explain: RNN text generators operate in a continuous loop: they take the text generated so far, predict the next word's probabilities, sample one word, and feed it right back into the input for the next cycle.
